Rbebelm 0.3.6-0.1.0
  • Reference

    • Backends and webR
    • Benchmark harness

Changelog

Source: NEWS.md

Rbebelm 0.3.6-0.1.0

  • Replaced the misleading embedding API with bebel_pooled_states() and bebel_token_states(). Extracted states now receive the model’s final output RMSNorm, pooled states default to SGPT-style position-weighted means, and result metadata explicitly records that the causal LFM weights are not retrieval-trained.
  • Renamed the corresponding option classes and native methods around contextual states; the experimental package does not retain the former embedding names.
  • Fixed AArch64 scalar and NEON artifacts to avoid dot-product instructions; the dot-product artifact alone is compiled with that CPU feature. Added an AArch64 cross-build check for all runtime-dispatch variants.
  • Exposed the existing "dotprod" backend as an explicit R selection.
  • Refocused the package on native BebeLM model loading, tokenization, contextual states, generation, persistent agents, R tool calls, and async jobs.
  • Removed the R-side agent framework, interactive frontend, session tree, extension layer, provider registry, and fuzzy-file-search code.
  • Added S7 classes with property validators for model options, agent options, generation options, contextual-state options, tool specs, and native pointer refs.
  • Added async model and agent generation through Rust worker threads. Jobs are represented as BebelAsyncJob objects and collected from R with bebel_async_collect().
  • Removed the model execution mutex so async jobs can run concurrently while sharing immutable memory-mapped weights and keeping independent caches.
  • Surfaced the shared-weight model design: agents own transcript/decode state while sharing loaded GGUF weights through the Rust backend.
  • Updated README, vignettes, pkgdown metadata, tinytests, and the webR package-load check around real BebeLM runs and the lean public API.
  • Hardened native SIMD dispatch: scalar builds use a baseline target and optimized dylibs are selected by runtime CPU checks.
  • Moved pooled states onto Rust batched paths: long texts use token-chunk prefill batching, and text vectors use independent sequence batching so short inputs can share batched matmul work. bebel_pooled_states() exposes token_batch_size and sequence_batch_size.
  • Kept batched state extraction memory-bounded by processing text chunks to completion with at most sequence_batch_size live sequence caches.

Rbebelm 0.0.0.9000

  • Initial experimental scaffold.

On this page

Developed by Sounkou Mahamane Toure.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.1.
